title: hieracium murorum subsp. subsanguineum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subsanguineum, also known as bloodsucker hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like genus of plants, characterized by its red-tinged leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. subsanguineum include its ability to act as a blood thinner and stimulate circulation, making it useful in the treatment of conditions such as varicose veins and poor circulation. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium murorum subsp. subsanguineum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused in oil for topical application, or taken as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
